pages:
Pathfinder series: Breast Cancer  
This pathfinder provides information about resources available in the  
JCC Patient & Family Resource Centre and on the internet.  
 Books  
 Breast Cancer and You: A Guide for Women Living with Breast Cancer / Katia Tonkin.  
Call number: 7.4.12 2011  
This guide was written to help the patient diagnosed with breast cancer find the information and 
help they need. It provides information about the staging of the disease, the likelihood of a cure 
and treatment options, practical in formation about procedures and resources, as well as 
information about coping with the emotional effects of cancer on themselves and their families.  
 
Breast Cancer Clear and Simple: all your questions answered  / American Cancer Society  
Call number: 7.4.45 2016  
You've just found out you have breast cancer and you need easy -to-understand answers to lots 
of questions. This book from the American Cancer Society will help you understand treatment 
choices, deal with the effects of treatment, find out about suppor t groups and how to move on 
after treatment. There are lists of suggested questions to ask your health care team at the 
various stages of treatment.  
 
 Breast Cancer Journey: The Essential Guide to Treatment and Recovery / Ruth O’Regan.  
 Call number: 7.4.21 2013  
 Presents a guide to coping with the physical and emotional demands of breast cancer treatment 
and recovery, in an updated edition that covers the latest treatment options, building a support 
network, and breast reconstruction.  
 
Dr. Sus an Love’s Breast Book  / Susan W. Love and Karen Lindsey.  
 Call number: 7.4.0 2015  
This is a comprehensive text about the breast. It provides information on the healthy breast, as 
well as on the diagnosis and treatment of breast cancer. The author is a phy sician with 
extensive experience in this field. She offers practical advice on maintaining quality -of-life during 
treatment.  
 
 The Breast Cancer Survival Manual / John Link.  
Call number: 7.4.6 2017  
   This book provides essential updates on treatment and care and includes advice on the new 
genomic classification of breast cancer, cancer gene testing, and the continued importance of 
getting a second opinion.  
  
              Mar/19

Websites  
 
BC Cancer Agency: Breast Cancer  
http://www.bccancer.bc.ca/health -info/types -of-cancer/breast -cancer   
The BC Cancer Agency provides a nice, comprehensive and straight forward guide to a 
breast cancer diagn osis. Throughout the site are links to other sources of information, 
definitions of terms and information about some of the BCCA’s programs and clinics as 
well. Skip to the bottom of the publication and there are links to other useful websites, 
videos and other multimedia resources.  
 
Breastcancer.org  http://www.breastcancer.org/   
Breastcancer.org is one of the first and still one of the best sites dedicated to providing 
the most reliable, complete, and up -to-date information about breast cancer. The site 
also boasts an active online community for people living with breast cancer, “Ask -the-
Expert” online conferences, a blog and more.  
 
Canadian Breast Cancer Network  http://www.cb cn.ca/   
The CBCN’s site provides news, events, and information for different types of patients 
and different types of breast cancer, including sections for young women, those with 
metastatic cancer, rural women, a section dealing with survivorship issues a nd one for 
those who have been newly diagnosed. Videos, a downloadable newsletter and links to 
other helpful resources are also included.  
 
Canadian Cancer Society – Breast Cancer  
http://www.cancer.ca/en/cancer -information/cancer -type/breast/breast -
cancer/?region=on  
The CCS has a good, detailed overview of breast cancer – types, treatments, grading and 
staging, supportive care information, and much more. As with most things cancer related, the 
CCS website or their excellent Cancer Information Service phone line (1 -888-939-3333) is a 
great place to start your research.  
  
Cedars MUHC Patient Education Guides – Breast Cancer  
http://www.muhcpatienteducation.ca/cancer -guides/find -a-cancer/breast -
cancer.html?parentID=300&sectionID=305  
These patient guides are an innovative approach to learning about what cancer is, how 
it is treated, what other effects it can have on the body (fertility, menopause, etc.) and 
more. Learn how breast cancer is staged, how it may spread, the different type s of 
surgery and other treatments that are available, as well as what you can expect after 
treatment.   
 
Living Beyond Breast Cancer  http://www.lbbc.org/  
This site offers information, support, and resources for breast ca ncer patients and 
survivors. Sections include information for the newly diagnosed, triple -negative breast 
cancer, LGBT, men with breast cancer, and more. Videos and multimedia sources are 
offered on the website as well.
